preamble: The phonetic transformation from the diphthong /au/ to /oi/ is a documented sound change observed in several language families, notably in Romance and Austronesian languages. This shift represents a significant alteration in the articulation of both components of the diphthong.
phoneticEffects: This shift typically involves a series of changes to both elements of the diphthong

examples.note: Proto-Austronesian *au shifted to oi in some Northwest Seram languages, presumably via *ai.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
